47023.description: PIPSC CALGARY BRANCH invites you to LABOUR DAY FAMILY EVENT Details: Join us for a fun filled day downhill karting at the Skyline Luge located at Winsport Canada Olympic Park. Each registered guest will receive chairlift access and 3 rides. Members can register to get an all-access pass for themselves, accompanying adult, and children, all of whom must reside at the same address. Please choose all passes for the same time slot. MAXIMUM OF 4 PASSES PER MEMBER. Registration opens at 7:00 am on Friday, 22 August 2025 and closes at 11:00 pm on Wednesday, 27 August 2025. The number of passes is limited so register early at: https://PIPSCCalgaryBranchFallFamilyEvent.eventbrite.ca Where: WinSport Canada Olympic Park located at 88 Canada Olympic Road SW, Calgary, AB When: Saturday, August 30, 2025 Come pick up your passes from a scheduled time slot between 11:00 a.m. until 11:30 a.m., 12:00 p.m. until 12:30 p.m., 1:00 p.m. until 1:30 p.m., or 2:00 p.m. until 2:30 p.m. We will only be handing out passes during these times. Please select only one time slot for you and your family. *****IMPORTANT INFORMATION REGARDING AGE, HEIGHT AND WEIGHT OF RIDERS******* Downhill Karting: Children must be at least 6 years of age and over 110cm tall to be eligible to ride unaccompanied. Children aged 2 years+ and over 85cm tall can ride the Karts in tandem with an adult aged 18+ years provided the child can walk unassisted, fit a helmet and follow our staff’s instructions. Please note both Kart driver

55697.description: Your NR Hamilton-Burlington Sub-Group plans to hold a lunch and learn information session from the Professional Engineers of Ontario (PEO) by the PEAK team, who will be delivering this session. The meeting will include a brief description of the program ————————————————————- PEO practitioners are required to complete continuing professional development (CPD) every year to maintain their licence. PEO administers this requirement through the Practice Evaluation and Knowledge (P.E.A.K.) program, which helps licence holders avoid competency drift and practise competently and ethically. 60436.title: September 10 is World Suicide Prevention Day!
60436.description: September 10 is World Suicide Prevention Day! Dear colleagues and friends, Each year on September 10, World Suicide Prevention Day reminds us of the impact we can have by simply showing up – for ourselves and others. You do not need to be a therapist to make a difference. Checking-in can help someone feel seen, supported and safe enough to seek help. That’s why open, empathetic conversations matter. They reduce stigma, foster connection, and create pathways to care. Consider taking a moment to learn more through the Canadian Association for Suicide Prevention website and make use of their promotional materials in honor of World Suicide Prevention Day. This year’s theme is once again ‘Changing the Narrative on Suicide’. Changing the narrative is about shifting from a culture of silence and stigma to one of openness, understanding and support. Please also promote these resources: If you or someone else appears to be in immediate danger, please call 9-1-1. If you or someone you know is thinking about suicide, call or text 9-8-8. Support is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week through 9-8-8: Suicide Crisis Helpline. Local crisis centres across Canada: Community Resources | 9-8-8: Suicide Crisis Helpline (988.ca). Filter by province to access regional help and assistance. Kids Help Phone @ 1-800-668-6868 or text TALK to 686868: Get support with these mental health resources – Kids Help Phone Indigenous people across Canada: Hope for Wellness Help Line @ 1-855-242-3310. 63594.title: Media Advisory — Dining table installation exposes the cost of cutting food safety experts
location: Corner of Sparks and Elgin Streets, Ottawa Speakers: Sean O’Reilly, President, Professional Institute of the Public Service of Canada Stéphanie Fréchette, PIPSC
63594.description: MEDIA ADVISORY | PHOTO OPPORTUNITY OTTAWA, February 12, 2026 — The Professional Institute of the Public Service of Canada (PIPSC) will stage a striking public installation to show what federal cuts to food safety really mean for Canadians. A family dining table will be set with recently recalled food items. A food safety scientist, a Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) expert, will be seated at the table representing the oversight and protection of Canadians food supply. Behind them, a large “receipt” will itemize what’s being cut: inspections, surveillance, and nearly one million hours of food safety expertise every year. The action sounds the alarm over federal cuts to CFIA, warning that food safety, public health, and Canada’s agri-food economy are being put at serious risk in the name of “efficiency.” The latest cuts at the CFIA are underscored by years of understaffing and capacity erosion. Event Details What: Visual media event – The cost of cutting food safety When: Friday, February 13, 2026, at 12:00 PM Where: Corner of Sparks and Elgin Streets, Ottawa Speakers: Sean O’Reilly, President, Professional Institute of the Public Service of Canada Stéphanie Fréchette, PIPSC Vice-President and CFIA professional PIPSC represents over 85,000 public-sector professionals across the country, most of them employed by the federal government. -30- For more information: Johanne Fillion, 613-883-4900 (mobile), jfillion@pipsc.ca —

66705.description: OTTAWA, March 11, 2026 — Sean O’Reilly, President of the Professional Institute of the Public Service of Canada (PIPSC), will appear before the House of Commons Standing Committee on Government Operations and Estimates (OGGO) as part of its study of the federal government’s Comprehensive Expenditure Review (CER). Published March 12, 2026 | Last updated 4 months ago O’Reilly will raise with Committee members the way federal spending cuts and workforce reductions will weaken Canada’s internal expertise and undermine the delivery of public services. 70840.title: Let’s rally for public services in Montreal
location: Corner of Viger and Saint-Urbain streets, Montreal, Quebec
70840.description: Public service workers are rallying in Montreal to make our work visible and show Canadians what’s at stake. Published March 31, 2026 | Last updated 3 months ago Public service workers from across the region are coming together in Montreal, outside the Liberal Party Convention, to make our work visible and show Canadians what’s at stake. Across the country, experts in the public service deliver critical programs and services that Canadians rely on every day. The latest Liberal budget has cut scientists, engineers, and policy experts whose work underpins dangerous goods regulation, rail safety, weather forecasting, pollution prevention, marine conservation, habitat restoration, and environmental disaster response. It takes experts to run a country. When experts are cut, risk increases. Join us in Montreal and stand up for the experts who protect the services Canadians rely on every day. Public service workers have always shown up for Canadians. Now it’s time to show up for each other. When:Saturday, April 11, 2026 For members travelling from the NCR, buses are available leaving at 8:30 AM. A light lunch will be served on the bus. Buses will depart Montreal at 1:30 PM, returning to Ottawa in the afternoon. Published July 6, 2026 | Last updated 1 week ago Join us for a dine-out series of events hosted by the SP 401 Burrard Sub-Group, the SP Lower Mainland BC Sub-Group, and the BC/Yukon Young Professionals Committee! Members of all ages are welcome. When: Wednesday, August 19, 2026, at 5:30 PMWhere: Torafuku, a Michelin-recommended restaurant, 958 Main St, Vancouver, BC Cost: PIPSC will cover up to $50 of each member’s meal.
